rationale: Neither candidate surfaces the one substantive ground-truth concern (yenuo26's question about verifying reduce_scatter behavior, not just hasattr, in the test), so recall is near-zero for both. X does more real work: concrete, file/line-anchored findings (missing pre-hook integration test, dp_metadata caching assumption, relaxed cfg_parallel_size guard) that are grounded in the diff, though some are repetitive restatements of the same blocking-collective observation. Y is mostly a confirmatory checklist ending in APPROVE with a single non-blocking comment overlapping X's caching point — accurate and well-calibrated (matches the real reviewers' LGTM) but far thinner and less actionable overall.
